perturbation.original_text: 1.2 "Banner Advertisement" means advertisements consisting of billboard-like graphics displayed in a standardized specific location on the Intuit Sites, which advertisements click-through to the Client Site, or such other address mutually agreed upon by the parties from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 "Banner Advertisement" means advertisements consisting of billboard-like graphics displayed in a standardized specific location on the Intuit Sites, which advertisements click-through to the Client Site, or such other address solely determined by Intuit.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Banner Advertisement' specifies that the click-through address is mutually agreed upon. This change gives Intuit the sole authority to determine the click-through address, creating a contradiction if the agreement elsewhere assumes mutual agreement on such matters. This affects Client's control over where their ads direct users.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Promotions. Commencing on the Launch Date and continuing throughout the ---------- Term, Intuit shall promote Client on the "Small Business" Channel of the Quicken.com Site, Excite Money & Investing Site and WebCrawler Money & Investing Site as follows: 2.1.1 A Sponsor Client Graphic consisting of 160x40 pixels shall be rotated amongst the following pages (or their successor pages, if any): (1) "Starting a Business" page, (2) "Managing your Business" page, (3) "Marketing" page, (4) "Legal Issues" page, and (5) "Taxes & Accounting" page. Each such Sponsor Client Graphic shall be Above the Fold. 2.1.2 A Sponsor Client Graphic of 88x31 pixels to be found at the bottom of each page where a sponsorship strip exists. 2.1.3 A text Link to be located Above-the-Fold in a text sponsor bar on the "Small Business" Channel home page of the Quicken.com Site, Excite Money & Investing Site and WebCrawler Money & Investing Site. 2.1.4 A text Link on the "Products & Promos" area of the "Small Business" Channel Home Page of the Quicken.com Site, Excite Money & Investing Site and WebCrawler Money & Investing Site. 2.1.5 A Sponsor Client Graphic consisting of a minimum number of pixels mutually agreed upon by the parties, will appear on the "Small Business Mailing/Shipping, OnLine Postage" page in the "Small Business" Channel, when such page is made publicly available on the applicable Intuit Sites. Such graphic shall be displayed in a position mutually agreed upon by the parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies mutual agreement for the click-through address of banner advertisements. The changed text grants Intuit sole discretion over this address. Section 2.1.5 of the contradicted text states that the minimum number of pixels and the position of a Sponsor Client Graphic will be 'mutually agreed upon by the parties'. By giving Intuit sole control over the click-through address, the modified text contradicts the requirement of mutual agreement specified in Section 2.1.5, creating a direct conflict and making the original agreement unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 8.4 Reports. Intuit or its Authorized Advertisement Server will provide Client ------- with monthly reports ("Usage Reports") substantiating the number of Impressions of Client's Banner Advertisements, Client Graphics, Sponsor Client Graphics and Links displayed on the Intuit Sites, the total number of click-throughs generated by each such advertisement or graphic, and such other information as the parties shall mutually agree.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.4 Reports. Intuit or its Authorized Advertisement Server may, at its discretion, provide Client ------- with reports ("Usage Reports") estimating the number of Impressions of Client's Banner Advertisements, Client Graphics, Sponsor Client Graphics and Links displayed on the Intuit Sites, the approximate number of click-throughs generated by each such advertisement or graphic, and such other information as Intuit deems relevant.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates Intuit to provide monthly reports substantiating the impressions and click-throughs, with details agreed upon by both parties. The revised text makes the reports discretionary, provides estimations rather than substantiated figures, and Intuit solely decides what information is relevant. This introduces conflict with sections that rely on accurate reporting for payment or performance evaluation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.2 Transaction Fees. Separate and apart from the fees in Subsection 10.1 above, at such time as Client has acquired [***] New Customers (the --- "Minimum Customer Number") Client will pay Intuit [***] of the Net --- Transaction Revenues it receives from each New Customer acquired by Client above the Minimum Customer Number ("Transaction Fee"). Within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month, Client will provide a monthly report (the "Transaction Fee Report") to Intuit. The Transaction Fee Report will report the Net Transaction Revenue and Transaction Fees for such month. All Transaction Fees due per the Transaction Fee Report will be paid with the submission of such Report.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates Intuit to provide monthly reports substantiating impressions and click-throughs. The changed text makes the reports discretionary, provides estimations rather than substantiated figures, and Intuit solely decides what information is relevant. This directly contradicts section 10.2, which requires 'Transaction Fee Report' from the Client to Intuit. Since Intuit is not required to provide any reports to the Client now in the Changed Text, the Client would have no basis to create this 'Transaction Fee Report' mentioned in Section 10.2, thereby creating a direct in-text contradiction. The changed text creates ambiguity in an area where the contradicted clause needs certainty (accurate reporting), resulting in a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 12.2 Termination. Either party may terminate this Agreement if the other party ----------- materially breaches a material obligation hereunder and such breach remains uncured for thirty (30) days following the notice to the breaching party of the breach and the notifying party's intention to terminate. All undisputed payments that have accrued prior to the termination or expiration of this Agreement for any reason will be payable in full within thirty (30) days thereof. In addition, upon the termination of this Agreement by Client for any reason, a pro-rata amount of the Monthly Payment Fee shall be refunded to Client calculated as follows: the Monthly Payment Fee less the cost of the Impressions displayed for such month as of the effective date of the termination, calculated on an average CPM basis.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.2 Termination. Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time, for any reason, with ten (10) days written notice to the other party. All undisputed payments that have accrued prior to the termination or expiration of this Agreement for any reason will be payable in full within ninety (90) days thereof. In addition, upon the termination of this Agreement by Client for any reason, no amount of the Monthly Payment Fee shall be refunded to Client .
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines termination as occurring due to a material breach with a 30-day cure period, and upon the termination of this Agreement by Client for any reason, a pro-rata amount of the Monthly Payment Fee shall be refunded to Client. This perturbation allows termination for any reason with a short notice period, contradicts other clauses outlining specific performance requirements and expected terms of engagement. It also states no amount of the Monthly Payment Fee shall be refunded to Client. Thus contradicting what was defined before of a pro-rata amount being refunded to Client.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.1 Term. Unless otherwise terminated as specified in this Section 12, the ---- term of this Agreement shall begin on the Effective Date and will not end until the later of (a) twelve (12) months from the Launch Date; or (2) the date Intuit displays a total of 176,717,916 Impressions in accordance with the terms set forth herein ("Term").
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text and the contradicted text (12.1) specify a term length of at least twelve months or until a specified number of impressions are displayed. The changed text allows termination 'at any time, for any reason, with ten (10) days written notice', directly contradicting the minimum term outlined in 12.1. This makes the agreement's intended minimum term unenforceable, fitting the definition of an 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'. Also, the change from 'pro-rata amount of the Monthly Payment Fee shall be refunded' to 'no amount of the Monthly Payment Fee shall be refunded to Client' is a direct, irreconcilable contradiction within the same section (12.2), reinforcing the 'YES' determination.
